channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Solidigm exits consumer SSD market as it prepares to go very big on enterprise SSDs in 2025
item.description: Solidigm's exit from consumer SSDs marks a significant shift, as it pivots towards enterprise storage.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Australian Open LIVE: tennis stream, cheapest deals, schedule, watch every match online, draw
item.description: Keen to watch the Australian Open 2025? Here are the best streams, the cheapest prices and how to watch every round from Melbourne as Jannik Sinner, Casper Ruud, Aryna Sabalenka and Novak Djokovic shoot for the title.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The TikTok ban is being weighed by the Supreme Court. Here’s what to know
item.description: The Supreme Court of the United States is hearing arguments today to decide the fate of TikTok. Lawmakers believe the app, which is owned by the Chinese company ByteDance, poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness
item.description: Current and former executives say the federal technology themes for 2025 will be similar to 2024 with the addition of efficiency and effectiveness. The post For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: IRS ‘prepared’ for 2025 filing season, but potential spending cuts loom for the agency
item.description: For the 2025 tax filing season, the IRS is expanding several modernization efforts. But potential budget cuts on the table could curtail those efforts. The post IRS ‘prepared’ for 2025 filing season, but potential spending cuts loom for the agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: JavaScript Trademark Battle Heats Up: Oracle Refuses to Yield
item.description: Deno Land's efforts to free up the JavaScript trademark from Oracle face a setback, with Oracle refusing to voluntarily withdraw its claim. The dispute could drag on for several months.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: 4 in 10 companies plan to replace employees with AI, WEF says
item.description: Forty-one percent of companies intend to cut their workforce in the next five years as many tasks are automated with AI, according to the World Economic Forum (WEF) Future of Jobs Report 2025.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New malware justifies Apple’s locked-down security strategy
item.description: Apple has told us Macs aren’t secure enough and it continues working to improve their security, as it does across all of its platforms. But a newly identified malware attack confirms that third-party developers can sometimes be a weak link in the perimeter.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok could be banned this month. Here's what users can do to prepare
item.description: TikTok has cemented itself as the quintessential entertainment app, offering everything from funny skits and makeup tutorials to social commentary and news.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: I’m a big PC gamer – these were the 3 best gaming handhelds announced at CES 2025
item.description: We've waited a while to see the veil lifted on the future of handheld gaming, and CES 2025 has done just that.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The future of customer experience: AI-powered technical documentation
item.description: Discover how AI can instantly decode technology and automates the creation of interactive documentation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More than 4% PC shipment growth predicted for 2025, but not for what you expect, says IDC
item.description: PC sales certainly weren’t going gangbusters in 2024: They only grew a paltry 1% over 2023. According to new figures from IDC, vendors shipped 262.7 million PCs in 2024. But things did pick up a bit in Q4 2024: Shipments grew 1.8% over the prior year, reaching 68.9 million.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sony PlayStation is adding smell—yes, you read that right—to its games
item.description: Sony has unveiled a new gaming system that could allow PlayStation players to sniff their way through games like The Last of Us. Unveiled at CES 2025, the Future Immersive Entertainment Concept (FIEC) features a huge, room-size setup designed to push the boundaries of immersive gaming.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Google, Boeing join growing list of companies donating to Trump's inauguration
item.description: Google and Boeing have each given $1 million to President-elect Trump’s inaugural committee, joining a growing list of major corporations supporting the Jan. 20 event. “Google is pleased to support the 2025 inauguration, with a livestream on YouTube and a direct link on our homepage.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: OPM giving two big data platforms a much-needed facelift
item.description: Taka Ariga, the chief data officer at the Office of Personnel Management, said the agency is modernizing, better integrating the EHRI and FedScope databases. The post OPM giving two big data platforms a much-needed facelift first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: What to know as TikTok argues in front of Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok is hoping to invalidate a law that would force it to divest from its Chinese parent company or face a ban.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Musk-Trump relationship not a 'concern': Hyundai CEO
item.description: Hyundai CEO José Muñoz said he’s not concerned about the relationship between President-elect Trump and Tesla CEO Elon Musk, whose company is one of Hyundai’s biggest competitors. Instead, Munoz said in an interview with Bloomberg that he thinks their close relationship could benefit the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Data Breach Affects Thousands of Schools in Indianapolis Area
item.description: Multiple school corporations in the Indianapolis area have confirmed that unauthorized users accessed their student information system as part of a national data breach.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok’s fate arrives at Supreme Court in collision of free speech and national security
item.description: The Supreme Court will hear arguments over the fate of TikTok, a digital age cultural phenomenon that roughly half the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: TikTok's fate arrives at Supreme Court in collision of free speech and national security
item.description: In a collision between free speech and national security, the Supreme Court will hear arguments over the fate of TikTok, a digital age cultural phenomenon that roughly half the U.S. population uses for entertainment and information.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal cyber leaders on delivering innovation
item.description: Need innovation inspiration when it comes to cyber? The post Federal cyber leaders on delivering innovation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Top tech stealing the show at CES 2025
item.description: Big tech coming in 2025 includes solar umbrellas, AI TVs, smart earbuds and crazy robots. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his take on the wonders revealed at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gleam 1.7 brings faster record updates
item.description: Gleam 1.7 has arrived with faster record updates and a dynamic coder for a custom type. Overall, the latest version of Gleam touts performance and package publishing improvements. This latest update to Gleam was announced January 5 and is accessible from GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: Editor’s note: This is an archive of 2024 incidents as tracked by Cisco subsidiary ThousandEyes. For current trends, see the 2025 outage report and internet health check, which is updated weekly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How adding capacity to a network could reduce IT costs
item.description: Finding an enterprise looking to spend more on network equipment is like finding gold while digging for worms for bait. Cost management used to be an option in enhancing profits, but these days it’s a mantra.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Python wins Tiobe language of the year honors
item.description: To the surprise of probably no one, Python has won the Tiobe programming language of the year award for 2024, given to the language with the highest increase in ratings in Tiobe’s index of language popularity during the year.
item.dc:creator: Info World
